title: DPbD Lifecycle Integration
description: How DPbD is operationalised across the data processing lifecycle. Each stage has design-time decisions, technical/organisational measures, and evidence expectations.
lifecycleNote: The issued JPDP DPbD Guideline definition (Section 3.1) names FOUR lifecycle stages: design, development, deployment, decommissioning. The 'operation' stage between deployment and decommissioning is an analyst-added stage retained here for operational completeness — it covers continuous monitoring, periodic access reviews, and ongoing privacy maintenance which are required practice but not separately named in the issued definition.

stages.name: Design
objectives: Identify the lawful basis and specific purpose(s) before any processing is contemplated
Determine whether the purpose can be achieved without personal data, or with anonymised/aggregated data
Define data minimisation envelope: what data, what frequency, what retention
Build privacy considerations into requirements, user stories, system architecture, data models
Conduct a DPIA where the processing meets the DPIA quantitative or qualitative thresholds
techMeasures: Data segmentation and isolation between purposes
orgMeasures: DPbD checklist signed off at requirements gate
Threat model and attack surface analysis

stages.name: Development
objectives: Implement privacy controls as first-class features (not as an afterthought patch)
Default settings: most privacy-protective option pre-selected; opt-in for additional collection
Validation rules at point of collection (mandatory vs optional fields, format checks)
Build access controls (RBAC, least privilege) into the system from day one
Make data subject rights mechanisms (access, correction, portability, erasure, withdrawal) part of the system from day one
orgMeasures: Security testing including privacy test cases
Documentation of data flows and processing operations

stages.name: Deployment
objectives: Pre-launch DPIA / DPbD validation against design intent
Verify default settings before go-live (opt-in defaults, minimum collection)
Privacy notice deployed and visible at all collection touchpoints before any data is collected
Onboarding training for staff handling personal data
Breach response plan and incident playbooks operational
techMeasures: Production encryption keys provisioned and rotated
orgMeasures: Staff training records up to date

stages.name: Operation
objectives: Continuous monitoring of processing against the original purpose
Periodic accuracy reviews and rectification on data subject request
Periodic access reviews; deprovisioning on role change or termination
Incident detection and breach response readiness (drills, tabletops)
Regular audits of policy effectiveness; suggestions reviewed and adopted
orgMeasures: Annual DPIA refresh for high-risk processing

stages.name: Decommissioning
objectives: Identify all data stores (primary, replicas, backups, third-party copies, dev/test)
Securely destroy or anonymise personal data per the retention schedule
Notify third parties to whom data was disclosed of the cessation; require their secure deletion
Issue certificates of destruction; update RoPA to reflect cessation
Conduct lessons-learned review for next system
techMeasures: Cryptographic erasure or physical media destruction
orgMeasures: Authorised disposal record signed by data owner
